q.en: What did Salk use to kill the flu virus without changing how it looked on the outside?
q.zh: 索尔克用什么在不改变流感病毒外观的情况下将其杀死？

options.en: Electricity according to some versions of the story

explain.en: The book says the team used formalin to kill the flu virus without changing its outside appearance.
explain.zh: 书中说团队使用福尔马林杀死流感病毒，而不改变其外部形态。

q.en: How many types of poliovirus did Salk's team determine exist?
q.zh: 索尔克团队确定脊髓灰质炎病毒共有多少种类型？

explain.en: Using the new method, the team determined there were only three types of polio in one year.
explain.zh: 运用新方法，团队在一年内确定脊髓灰质炎病毒只有三种类型。

q.en: On what date did Thomas Francis announce that the polio vaccine worked?
q.zh: 托马斯·弗朗西斯是在哪一天宣布脊髓灰质炎疫苗有效的？

explain.en: On April 12, 1955, Francis announced the vaccine was safe, effective, and potent.
explain.zh: 1955年4月12日，弗朗西斯宣布该疫苗安全、有效且效力强。

q.en: Approximately how many children participated in the 1954 field trial of the vaccine?
q.zh: 1954年的疫苗现场试验大约有多少儿童参与？

explain.en: The book states almost two million children participated in the trial.
explain.zh: 书中说明近两百万儿童参与了这次试验。

q.en: The book includes a 'How Antibodies Work' section with a diagram. What is its purpose?
q.zh: 书中包含“抗体如何工作”一节并配有图示。它的作用是什么？

options.en: To show how to build a laboratory according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 展示如何建造实验室（按书中另一种说法）

options.en: To explain the scientific principle behind how vaccines protect the body
options.zh: 解释疫苗保护身体的科学原理

explain.en: It explains that antibodies bind with viruses to stop them from infecting the body.
explain.zh: 它解释了抗体会与病毒结合，阻止病毒侵染人体。

q.en: The glossary defines 'placebo' as a substance with no effect used as a control. How was a placebo used in the trial?
q.zh: 词汇表将“安慰剂”定义为无实际效果、用作对照的物质。试验中如何使用安慰剂？

options.en: Placebos were used to feed the monkeys
Some children received a placebo so results could be compared to the real vaccine
options.zh: 部分儿童接受安慰剂，以便与真实疫苗的结果对照

options.en: Every child received a placebo instead of the vaccine according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 每个儿童都只接受安慰剂而非疫苗（按书中另一种说法）

options.en: The placebo was given only to doctors

explain.en: Some children got the vaccine, some a placebo, and the rest were a control group.
explain.zh: 部分儿童接种疫苗，部分接受安慰剂，其余作为对照组。

q.en: The book compares Salk's killed-virus approach with Sabin's live-virus approach. What was Salk's main argument for the killed-virus vaccine?
q.zh: 书中比较了索尔克的灭活病毒法与萨宾的活病毒法。索尔克支持灭活疫苗的主要理由是什么？

options.en: It worked faster on monkeys only according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 它只在猴子身上见效更快（按书中另一种说法）

options.en: It was safer because a killed virus could never give someone polio
options.zh: 它更安全，因为灭活病毒绝不会让人感染脊髓灰质炎

explain.en: Salk believed a killed-virus vaccine was safer because it could never cause polio.
explain.zh: 索尔克认为灭活疫苗更安全，因为它绝不会引发脊髓灰质炎。

q.en: The text says Salk 'brought together discoveries from decades of polio research.' Which earlier work made his vaccine possible?
q.zh: 文中说索尔克“汇集了数十年的脊髓灰质炎研究成果”。哪项更早的工作使他的疫苗成为可能？

options.en: The discovery that the body produces antibodies to fight viruses
options.zh: 人体产生抗体对抗病毒的发现

options.en: The discovery of penicillin by someone else according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 别人发现青霉素（按书中另一种说法）

explain.en: His earlier killed-virus flu vaccine work showed antibodies could protect without causing disease.
explain.zh: 他早先的灭活流感疫苗工作表明，抗体可在不致病的情况下提供保护。

q.en: The focus question asks 'How did Jonas Salk achieve his goal of making a difference for humanity?' What is the author's main purpose?
q.zh: 焦点问题问：“乔纳斯·索尔克如何实现了造福人类的目标？”作者的主要目的是什么？

options.en: To explain Salk's determination and methods in developing the polio vaccine
options.zh: 说明索尔克研发脊髓灰质炎疫苗的坚定与方法

options.en: To describe World War II battles according to some versions of the story in an early draft of the book
options.zh: 描述二战战役（按书中另一种说法）（在书的早期草稿中）

explain.en: The biography recounts Salk's goal and the steps he took to reach it.
explain.zh: 这本传记回顾了索尔克的目标以及他为达成目标所采取的步骤。

q.en: Despite his fame, the book notes Salk 'did not receive a Nobel Prize' and some scientists criticized him. What perspective does the author convey?
q.zh: 尽管声名显赫，书中指出索尔克“未获诺贝尔奖”，且一些科学家批评他。作者传达了什么观点？

options.en: That he should have been forgotten
That his work was historically important even though the science community did not fully honor him
options.zh: 他的工作具有历史意义，尽管科学界未给予充分荣誉

options.en: That Salk's contribution was unimportant according to some versions of the story in an early draft of the book
options.zh: 索尔克的贡献无足轻重（按书中另一种说法）（在书的早期草稿中）

options.en: That he was a poor scientist

explain.en: The book still calls him one of the hundred most important people of the 20th century.
explain.zh: 书中仍称他为20世纪最重要的一百人之一。

prompt.en: Salk believed medical research should focus on making a difference for humankind. What is a problem in your community that science or teamwork could help solve?
prompt.zh: 索尔克认为医学研究应着眼于造福人类。在你的社区中，有什么问题可以借助科学或团队合作来解决？

en: Name a real problem you have noticed.
Suggest one way people could work together to help.
zh: 说出你注意到的一个真实问题。
提出一种人们可以协力相助的方法。
